assumptions: This task only changes documentation and navigation, not codex-cli runtime behavior.
The implementation document must describe current source behavior, even when older docs use stale wording.

risks: Long implementation docs can drift from source code; paths, function names, and contracts must be explicit.
Existing docs include older unified-diff-first language; the new document must state the current SEARCH/REPLACE primary path.
Documentation-only work cannot use a meaningful failing runtime test, so verification relies on Rust checks and link/path review.
